dominantJob.reasoning: While the primary action (asking a question) is present, the screen is overwhelmingly cluttered with empty state panels, sidebars, and pre-allocated answer structures that compete for the user's attention before they have even initiated an action.
violations: Left sidebar (Context, Evidence Cards, Stage Pills, Run Snapshot)
Pre-allocated answer boxes (What it is, Why it matters, What is missing, What to do next)
Secondary action buttons (Go deeper, Show risks, etc.) visible before content exists
Save/Open report buttons visible before a report is generated

visibleReasoning.reasoning: NodeBench excels here. It makes a strong structural commitment to transparency by dedicating persistent UI space to sources, agent traces, and run stages, ensuring the user doesn't have to blindly trust the AI output.
trustSignals: RUN SNAPSHOT showing quantitative metrics of the run

speedBehavior.reasoning: The UI uses 'PENDING' states and fixed-height boxes which strongly suggests progressive loading and layout stability. The system is designed to stream content into pre-defined areas without causing jarring layout shifts.
latencyIssues: The sheer number of panels might cause parallel loading bottlenecks
Static 'PENDING' text might feel unresponsive if the agent takes more than a few seconds to start streaming

qualityDiscipline.reasoning: The UI is high-fidelity with consistent dark mode tokens, typography, and spacing. However, the visual quality is degraded by the sheer volume of borders and dashed lines, which creates a noisy, wireframe-like appearance.
papercuts: Overuse of 1px borders creates visual noise
Dashed borders on empty states look unpolished compared to solid subtle fills
Vertical alignment of the arrow icon in the 'Send' button feels slightly off-center

contextCompounding.reasoning: There are strong, visible signals that the system uses past data and user context to tailor its outputs, moving away from the 'blank slate' problem of standard chatbots.
compoundingSignals: Persona selectors (Founder, Investor, Banker, etc.) below the chat input

chromeCollapse.reasoning: The interface fails this principle completely. Almost every single element, panel, and sub-panel is wrapped in a 1px border, creating a 'box-in-a-box' nightmare that destroys visual hierarchy.
excessChrome: 4 internal answer boxes (What it is, etc.)

components.name: Chat Input
components.beforeInteraction: Empty text box with placeholder 'Continue the live session...'
components.duringInteraction: Shows typing indicator or disabled state while generating
components.afterInteraction: Clears for the next prompt or allows editing
components.reasoning: This is the primary action but is buried at the bottom of a busy center panel. It should be the absolute focus of the empty state.

components.name: Pre-allocated Answer Boxes
components.beforeInteraction: Empty boxes showing 'PENDING' and structural titles
components.duringInteraction: Streams text into the respective boxes
components.reasoning: Showing empty structural boxes before a question is asked confuses the user and clutters the screen. These should only appear once generation begins.

components.name: Right Sidebar (Sources & Activity)
components.beforeInteraction: Empty dashed boxes with explanatory text
components.duringInteraction: Populates with links and agent logs progressively
components.afterInteraction: Displays a full list of sources and the complete agent trace
components.reasoning: Highly valuable for trust, but showing empty dashed boxes on initial load adds unnecessary cognitive load. Hide or collapse until the user submits a prompt.

components.name: Left Sidebar (Context & Stages)
components.beforeInteraction: Shows active context pills and empty state metrics
components.duringInteraction: Stage pills might light up, metrics update
components.afterInteraction: Shows final run stats and used context
components.reasoning: Takes up too much horizontal space for secondary information. Context could be consolidated into a dropdown or inline indicator near the chat input.

competitorComparison: Compared to ChatGPT or Perplexity, NodeBench's empty state is vastly overcomplicated, presenting a dashboard of empty panels rather than a focused search/chat input. However, NodeBench excels in visible reasoning, dedicating explicit, persistent UI space to agent traces and sources, which Perplexity hides behind clicks and ChatGPT largely obscures.
topIssues: Extreme visual clutter from excessive borders, violating the Chrome Collapse principle.
Empty state shows pre-allocated answer boxes (What it is, Why it matters) before a question is asked, confusing the user.
Sidebars (Context, Sources, Agent Activity) compete with the primary chat input for attention on initial load.
Too many secondary actions (Go deeper, Show risks, Save report, Open full report) are visible before any content is generated.
Dashed borders for empty states feel unpolished and add to the wireframe-like visual noise.
recommendations: Center the chat input vertically and horizontally on the initial load (like ChatGPT/Google) to create a single, undeniable primary action.
Remove all pre-allocated answer boxes ('What it is', etc.) from the empty state; only render them dynamically once generation begins.
Hide the right sidebar (Sources, Agent Activity) completely until the user submits a prompt.
Remove 1px borders from internal panels and use subtle background fills or whitespace to establish hierarchy.
Consolidate the left sidebar context pills into a single 'Context' dropdown or inline indicator attached to the chat input.
